introStatement: I am a collaborative and people-centered leader who values inclusivity, trust, and growth. I am passionate about creating environments where individuals feel heard, supported, and empowered to reach their full potential. Through my background in organizational leadership, caregiving, and mentorship, I bring empathy, clear communication, and a strong commitment to helping others succeed. I believe effective leadership is not just about guiding teams, but about uplifting others and creating meaningful, lasting impact.

education.degree: Bachelor of Applied Science, BAS-OLTM - Organizational Leadership & Technical Management
education.description: Focused on leadership, strategy, collaboration, and inclusive organizational growth.

education.degree: Associate of Applied Science in Medical Administration
education.description: Built strong administrative, communication, and healthcare systems knowledge.

workExperience.description: Serve as a peer mentor to 200+ college students, helping Scholars to navigate academic and professional resources, supporting them in college and WSOS processes, sharing opportunities, and building a sense of community.
Support quadriplegic patients with daily living activities, physical care, emotional well-being, and greater independence in the home environment.
Oversaw day-to-day operations, led a team of approximately 15 employees, managed hiring and training, and improved workflow, service quality, and inventory control.

skills: Ability to Multitask and Manage Multiple Projects

projects.title: COIL Project: Final Group Presentation on COIL & Social Media Influences
projects.description:
The Collaborative Online International Learning (COIL) Project was a five-week virtual collaboration between students from Lower Columbia College (LCC) and the University of Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates. This project focused on exploring cultural differences in digital identity and social media use, allowing us to compare perspectives shaped by distinct cultural and societal norms. Through cross-cultural collaboration, I developed stronger communication skills, particularly in navigating time zones, language nuances, and differing viewpoints. I also strengthened my leadership and teamwork skills by contributing to group discussions and coordinating our final presentation.

This project is especially relevant to my academic and professional development because it enhanced my cultural competence, which is an essential skill in today's globalized and diverse workplace. Understanding how culture influences communication and digital behavior prepares me to work more effectively in team environments, particularly in roles that require collaboration across diverse populations. This experience reinforced the importance of adaptability, empathy, and inclusive communication.

projects.title: Progressive Journal
projects.description: My Progressive Journal from High Performance is an important artifact because it reflects my growth as a collaborative leader. In this journal, I examine my strengths, including being organized, empathetic, and goal-oriented, while also identifying areas where I can continue to improve, such as patience and allowing others more opportunities to lead. This reflection is relevant to my capstone because it demonstrates self-awareness, adaptability, and a commitment to continuous growth, all of which are important qualities for effective leadership. It also shows that I value inclusive teamwork and understand the importance of creating an environment where all members feel heard and respected. Through experiences with diverse teams and virtual collaboration, I learned that successful leadership requires both direction and flexibility. This reflection connects directly to my leadership development because it highlights how I work with others, navigate conflict, and support shared goals. Overall, this artifact represents my ability to reflect on team dynamics and apply leadership concepts in practical, meaningful ways.

projects.title: Leadership Portfolio
projects.description: My Leadership Portfolio from Transformative Leadership and Decision-Making is an important artifact because it captures my personal philosophy of leadership and how it has developed through my academic and life experiences. In this portfolio, I explain that leadership is not about power, but about empowering others, creating an inclusive environment, and helping people reach their full potential. This artifact is especially relevant to my capstone because it reflects the values that guide my leadership: empathy, authenticity, trust, and adaptability. It also includes examples of leadership experiences that show my growth through mentorship, service, and group projects. One of the most valuable lessons from this portfolio is that leadership is a continuous learning process that requires reflection and the willingness to grow. This aligns with my capstone theme because it shows how my leadership style is rooted in supporting others, encouraging collaboration, and making thoughtful decisions that benefit the group.

projects.title: Workforce Communication Assessment
projects.description: My Workforce Communication Assessment from Organizational Communication is an important artifact because it shows my ability to analyze communication within an organization and identify ways to improve it. In this assessment, I examined the communication structure, culture, stakeholders, and challenges within the Cowlitz Youth Soccer Association. This artifact is relevant to my capstone because it demonstrates that I understand how communication affects trust, transparency, and organizational effectiveness. It also reflects my interest in inclusive communication and my ability to think critically about how organizations can better serve the people involved in them. Through this project, I learned how communication systems, leadership decisions, and stakeholder needs all shape the success of a group or organization. This connects to my leadership development because it shows that I not only value communication but also understand how to use it strategically to support positive change. Overall, this artifact highlights my skills in observation, analysis, and problem-solving in a real-world organizational setting.

projects.title: Shark Tank
projects.description: My Shark Tank project from Small Group Communication is relevant to my capstone because it demonstrates how effective communication and teamwork contribute to group success. In this project, my group established roles, set communication norms early, and worked together to complete the assignment effectively. I served as the coordinator, which allowed me to use my organizational skills and help keep the group on track. This artifact shows that I value preparation, shared responsibility, and clear communication in team settings. It also highlights my ability to adapt when our group lost a member, requiring us to redistribute tasks and adjust our plan. That experience reinforced the importance of flexibility, trust, and problem-solving in group communication. For my capstone, this project is meaningful because it reflects my ability to help build a productive and respectful team environment. It also shows that I can contribute to group success by staying organized, communicating clearly, and supporting others through change.
